params.prompt: Transform this room image into a cinematic editorial real-estate still. Preserve exact architecture, perspective, walls, windows, door openings, furniture layout, lens realism, and room scale. Maintain believable residential scale for a 7.6666666667 by 9.6666666667 ft Dining Room (Dine). High natural light from bay-style exterior windows on the right-side projection. Use the bay as a primary light source; add a single centered brass pendant to create a warm focal glow over the table for evening cues. A warm, layered mid‑century inspired home with an earthy palette anchored in emerald greens, reclaimed walnut, brass accents and tactile woven surfaces. The overall direction is calm and artistic — think botanical spa meets curated gallery: lush plants, handcrafted textures, and intentional pops of warm terracotta and ochre. Light should feel soft and directional from existing windows, sculpting materials rather than flattening them. Keep these fixed unless the user explicitly wants transformation: exact architecture room geometry furniture layout lens realism vertical line integrity believable scale light entering from visible sources only VISUAL CONSISTENCY REQUIREMENT (reference image provided): The dining table, pendant light above the dining area, dining chairs, round/oval wood dining table, brass pendant centered in bay, bay-window view with plants, textured runner and ceramic vignette, brass pendant, textured runner and a small ceramic vignette in this room must exactly match the style, materials, and form shown in reference image — treat it as the definitive source of truth for these pieces. The Kitchen (Taste) visible through the open kitchen must show exactly the same kitchen cabinets, range hood, kitchen island or counter, deep green vertical tile backsplash, walnut cabinetry and minimal brass hardware, marble/honed counter vignette (cutting board, small herb), under-cabinet warm strip lighting, walnut cabinet faces with minimal brass hardware, marble or honed stone counter vignette (cutting board, ceramics) as in the reference image — do not invent different furniture. The Living Room (Nest) visible through the open-plan living area must show exactly the same sofa, coffee table, area rug, bay-window view on right wall, bookshelf (curated), reclaimed walnut sofa and coffee table, large potted plant, brass floor lamp, reclaimed walnut sofa/coffee table, bookshelf (curated, non-cluttered), large potted plant (e.g., fiddle leaf fig), brass floor lamp or sconce for warm practical glow, bookshelf as in the reference image — do not invent different furniture. Previous failure modes to correct: major_style_mismatch, object_missing, lighting_unrealistic. The image generally aligns with the dining room prompt but has noticeable style discrepancies and missing elements like the specified textured runner and small ceramic vignette. The lighting is somewhat realistic, but does not conform to the high natural light instruction.
